title: "podman build" silently adds a variant (to local parent images)
body:
### Issue Description

While building images locally on my M2 (arm64) that are intended to run on linux/amd64 machines, I've encountered a strange phenomenon; in that (locally) built parent images were not found while attempting to build child images that depended on these.

Images were initially duly built by passing `--platform=linux/amd64` to `podman build`. Child images however could only be built by specifying `--platform=linux/amd64/v8` (note the nonexisting "v8" variant for amd64).

Interestingly enough, `skopeo inspect` does not reveal a variant, nor does `podman inspect`. Hence I assume something is lost (or rather, added) in translation, while `podman build` looks up the parent image. But I'm not versed enough in the inner workings of podman to interpret this conclusively.

This might be an earlier issue that has reappeared, the limited information and troubleshooting I've found did not really help.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create two containerfiles:

baseAFile:
```Dockerfile
FROM busybox
RUN pwd | tee pwd.txt
```

baseBFile:
```DockerFile
FROM myprefix/base-a:latest
RUN echo works
```

2. Build the first image: `podman build --platform=linux/amd64 -t myprefix/base-a:latest -f baseAFile`
```
STEP 1/2: FROM busybox
STEP 2/2: RUN pwd | tee pwd.txt
/
COMMIT myprefix/base-a:latest
--> 82ea99988343
Successfully tagged localhost/myprefix/base-a:latest
82ea9998834312e87631dbcb9bcc46ad1103656de48738ae5abfddb3a696f38c
```

4. Attempt to build the second image: `podman build --pull=never --platform=linux/amd64 -t myprefix/base-b:3 -f baseBFile`
```
podman build --pull=never --platform=linux/amd64 -t myprefix/base-b:3 -f baseBFile
STEP 1/2: FROM myprefix/base-a:latest
Error: creating build container: myprefix/base-a:latest: image not known
```


By manually adjusting the platform the second image can be built:
```
podman build --pull=never --platform=linux/amd64/v8 -t myprefix/base-b:3 -f baseBFile
STEP 1/2: FROM myprefix/base-a:latest
STEP 2/2: RUN echo works
--> Using cache c5d7958ac4c284becbeae66d549af99643cbae5cbaddc0b054dba3a54cdb1ebb
COMMIT myprefix/base-b:3
--> c5d7958ac4c2
Successfully tagged localhost/myprefix/base-b:3
c5d7958ac4c284becbeae66d549af99643cbae5cbaddc0b054dba3a54cdb1ebb
```



### Describe the results you received

Second image is not built because the first image cannot be found.

### Describe the results you expected

Second image is built

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

comments.body: @nalind @flouthoc Another variant issue, not sure if this is the same as https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/6185

It looks like the base image carries a variant field in its config (confirm: `skopeo inspect --override-arch=arm64 --override-os=linux --config --raw docker://busybox`), and we're preserving it when we base an image on it.

I'm not able to reproduce the "image not found" error from the second build on my linux/amd64 system or a linux/arm64 VM (natively, or remote talking to its local podman service), though.

Do you have output from the second build with `--log-level=debug`?  Maybe that'll shed some light on what's missing when I try to reproduce it here.

Sure, here you go (some internal repositories redacted):
```
podman build --log-level=debug --pull=never --platform=linux/amd64 -t myprefix/base-b:3 -f baseBFile
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug
DEBU[0000] Called build.PersistentPreRunE(podman build --log-level=debug --pull=never --platform=linux/amd64 -t myprefix/base-b:3 -f baseBFile)
DEBU[0000] SSH Ident Key "<redacted>/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/machine" <redacted>
DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: GET URI: http://d/v5.5.0/libpod/_ping
DEBU[0000] Loading registries configuration "<redacted>/.config/containers/registries.conf"
DEBU[0000] Found credentials for <redacted_reg_A> in credential helper containers-auth.json in file <redacted>/.config/containers/auth.json
DEBU[0000] Found credentials for <redacted_reg_B> in credential helper containers-auth.json in file <redacted>/.config/containers/auth.json
DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: POST URI: http://d/v5.5.0/libpod/build
STEP 1/2: FROM myprefix/base-a:latest
Error: creating build container: myprefix/base-a:latest: image not known

DEBU[0000] Shutting down engines
```

Interestingly, I am not seeing a variant field when inspecting `skopeo inspect --config containers-storage:busybox`, neither with nor without the --override flags. On `skopeo inspect --config containers-storage:localhost/myprefix/base-a` however, the variant field is present, both on arm64 and amd64. Perhaps this is spuriously injected in the first build step? (build host is an M2, which should result to arm64/v8 per my understanding).

The "docker://" storage url seems to be very slow currently, so I will provide additional output from skopeo when my TZ reaches daytime again. Though it is my understanding (and intent) that base-a and base-b are not published to an external registry.

Hang on, I think I got thrown by the similarity in spellings between arm64 and amd64.  The "v8" variant part of the host's platform information should be getting discarded when the target platform is specified, but it isn't.

I suspected as much :)

For planning, do I understand the PR correctly that a 1.40.x release is intended; fixing this behaviour?
That's my intent, yes.  Pending reviews, of course.
We just tagged v1.40.1, for anyone who's looking for it.

Looking into how to get manually into my workflow, as I suppose podman will need some time until their next release incorporating that includes buildah > 1.40. 

Thanks for the fix @nalind !
Podman 5.5.1 with that fix included will be releases today.
@Luap99 thx for the heads-up, in that case I'll wait until everything has percolated, as I expect a podman release will be fairly quickly picked up by homebrew.
